name: 99 - Master Coverage Suite (one per failure-mode category)
description: One representative question per category from the 79-case projection/selection/decomposition failure-mode master list. Tests the K1-tightened eval (every case has ExpectedSql or ExpectedSqlContains so IsVerified=true) and exercises every fix shipped in this batch. Phrasing is grounded against this app's real seed data — entity names, status/priority vocab, user emails — so results are reproducible.

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets do we have?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show open tickets.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS Status FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show me all the information about ticket TCK-2026-00001.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.Description, t.PriorityId, t.StatusId, t.CategoryId, t.EntityId, t.SourceId, t.AssignedToUserId, t.CreatedByUserId, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.TicketNumber = 'TCK-2026-00001' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show full details of the user with email admin@supportflow.local.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName, u.Email, u.FirstName, u.LastName, u.EntityId, u.IsActive FROM AspNetUsers u WHERE u.Email = 'admin@supportflow.local'

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets.
Scenarios.Category: A3 / Default projection pulls FK labels
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS Status, p.Name AS Priority, c.Name AS Category FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId JOIN TicketCategories c ON c.Id = t.CategoryId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: List entities.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT e.Name FROM Entitys e WHERE e.IsActive = 1 ORDER BY e.Name

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets together with their parent ticket's title.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.TicketNumber AS ParentNumber, p.Title AS ParentTitle FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN Tickets p ON p.Id = t.ParentTicketId WHERE t.ParentTicketId IS NOT NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show every descendant ticket of TCK-2026-00001.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: WITH descendants AS (SELECT Id FROM Tickets WHERE ParentTicketId = (SELECT Id FROM Tickets WHERE TicketNumber = 'TCK-2026-00001') UNION ALL SELECT t.Id FROM Tickets t JOIN descendants d ON t.ParentTicketId = d.Id) SELECT * FROM descendants

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with their tag names.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, tg.Name AS Tag FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketTagMappings m ON m.TicketId = t.Id JOIN TicketTags tg ON tg.Id = m.TagId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket counts per assignee (include unassigned).
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName AS Assignee, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.AssignedToUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY Count DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets and the role name of the user they are assigned to.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, u.UserName AS Assignee, r.Name AS Role FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.AssignedToUserId LEFT JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON ur.UserId = u.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many distinct users created tickets?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT t.CreatedByUserId) AS Count FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show the most recent ticket in each entity.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: WITH ranked AS (SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, e.Name AS Entity, t.CreatedAt, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY t.EntityId ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC) AS rn FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0) SELECT TicketNumber, Title, Entity, CreatedAt FROM ranked WHERE rn = 1

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets are critical and how many are low priority?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN p.Name = 'Critical' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Critical, SUM(CASE WHEN p.Name = 'Low' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Low FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show categories with more than 10 tickets.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT c.Name AS Category, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON c.Id = t.CategoryId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY c.Name HAVING COUNT(*) > 10 ORDER BY Count DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket counts grouped by the day they were created (last 90 days).
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT CAST(t.CreatedAt AS DATE) AS Day, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt >= DATEADD(day, -90, GETDATE()) AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY CAST(t.CreatedAt AS DATE) ORDER BY Day

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket counts grouped by status id.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT s.Id AS StatusId, s.Name AS Status, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY s.Id, s.Name ORDER BY Count DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket counts per priority and status.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT p.Name AS Priority, s.Name AS Status, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name, s.Name ORDER BY Priority, Status

Scenarios.Question: What is the total attachment size?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT SUM(CAST(a.FileSize AS FLOAT)) / 1048576.0 AS [Total (MB)] FROM TicketAttachments a

Scenarios.Question: Show monthly ticket counts for the last 12 months.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT FORMAT(t.CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') AS Month, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt >= DATEADD(month, -12, GETDATE()) AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY FORMAT(t.CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') ORDER BY Month

Scenarios.Question: Show 5 tickets with their age in days.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 5 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, DATEDIFF(day, t.CreatedAt, GETDATE()) AS AgeDays FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Which categories had tickets in April but not in May 2026?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT DISTINCT c.Name FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON c.Id = t.CategoryId WHERE t.CreatedAt >= '2026-04-01' AND t.CreatedAt < '2026-05-01' AND t.IsDeleted = 0 EXCEPT SELECT DISTINCT c.Name FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON c.Id = t.CategoryId WHERE t.CreatedAt >= '2026-05-01' AND t.CreatedAt < '2026-06-01' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Which users have created tickets in every category?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName FROM AspNetUsers u WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TicketCategories c WHERE c.IsActive = 1 AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id AND t.CategoryId = c.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0))

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket counts per assignee.
Scenarios.Category: F1 / NULL bucket gets a label
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT ISNULL(u.UserName, '(Unassigned)') AS Assignee, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.AssignedToUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY ISNULL(u.UserName, '(Unassigned)') ORDER BY Count DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show 10 tickets with their resolution time in hours, showing 0 when not yet resolved.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 10 t.TicketNumber, ISNULL(DATEDIFF(hour, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt), 0) AS ResolutionHours FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket counts as a matrix of priority rows and status columns.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT p.Name AS Priority, [Open], [Closed], [Resolved], [In Progress], [Pending], [New], [Rejected] FROM (SELECT p.Name AS Priority, s.Name AS Status FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0) src PIVOT (COUNT(*) FOR Status IN ([Open],[Closed],[Resolved],[In Progress],[Pending],[New],[Rejected])) AS pvt

Scenarios.Question: Show each ticket and its tag names as a single comma-separated list.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, STRING_AGG(tg.Name, ', ') AS Tags FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketTagMappings m ON m.TicketId = t.Id LEFT JOIN TicketTags tg ON tg.Id = m.TagId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY t.TicketNumber

Scenarios.Question: Show the latest ticket.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 1 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Who is the best support agent?
Show the most recent ticket per user.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: WITH ranked AS (SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, u.UserName, t.CreatedAt, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY t.CreatedByUserId ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC) AS rn FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.CreatedByUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0) SELECT TicketNumber, Title, UserName, CreatedAt FROM ranked WHERE rn = 1

Scenarios.Question: Show the top 3 newest tickets.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 3 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show the top 5 users by ticket count.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 5 u.UserName, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.CreatedByUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY Count DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many issues do we have?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show me each user's salary.
Show tickets where weight is greater than 5.
Show me the name.
Show all ticket statuses.
Scenarios.Category: I8 / Lookup-only retrieval + bidirectional FK expand
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT s.Name FROM TicketStatuses s WHERE s.IsActive = 1 ORDER BY s.Name

Scenarios.Question: List users with their emails.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u

Scenarios.Question: Show 10 tickets with their status name and priority name.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 10 t.TicketNumber, s.Name AS Status, p.Name AS Priority FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show me 5000 tickets.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 1000 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets whose title does not contain 'login'.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title NOT LIKE '%login%' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show me open tickets by category and closed.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT c.Name AS Category, s.Name AS Status, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON c.Id = t.CategoryId JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name IN ('Open', 'Closed') AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY c.Name, s.Name ORDER BY Category, Status

Scenarios.Question: How many users and how many roles do we have?
Users with no tickets assigned.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName FROM AspNetUsers u WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t WHERE t.AssignedToUserId = u.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0)

Scenarios.Question: List distinct ticket categories that have at least one ticket.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT DISTINCT c.Name FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON c.Id = t.CategoryId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show the average resolution time in hours by priority.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT p.Name AS Priority, AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(hour, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) AS AvgResolutionHours FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name

Scenarios.Question: Delete ticket TCK-2026-00001.
Show me all users; DROP TABLE Tickets; --
